text:
Sunaka (スナカ, Sunaka), also called Sunaka City (スナカの町, Sunaka no Machi) is a game-exclusive area in the Wonderswan games Hunter × Hunter: Those Who Inherit Will and Hunter × Hunter: Each One's Determination. It is said to be in the country of the same name. Envyplays “Hunter × Hunter: Those Who Inherit Will Scans” Tumblr. 

Overview

Sunaka is the city that Kurapika visits during Each One’s Determination in order to find a flesh collector job during the game's events. Naturally, this means the Sengi Guild is found there. Hisoka later visits in his fourth chapter. Various other businesses, such as a bar and internet cafe, can be found. There is also an abundance of restaurants such as Burger Kong, The town is noted for its presence of the Four Heavenly Kings, specifically Rorgo and Sansho, and the Sunaka Royal Family, such as Princess Clario. From the former’s presence, the city has a heavy crime rate that is unable to be controlled. According to Hisoka, Dore is close by.

Plot

Those Who Inherit Will

Each One's Determination

 Note: Events occurring in Each One's Determination do not constitute canon material. 

During Kurapika’s route, after promising Gon and company to meet at Yorknew City in September, he sets out to find a flesh collector employer interested in the Scarlet Eyes. His search leads him to Sunaka City. During his time there, he seeks out hints to the guild's location. His search results in him running into a cloaked figure named Clario. As he visits a bar, the bartender gives him a hint. This results in him going to a local internet cafe. Doing so, he is shocked to discover information regarding an eyeball collector named Rorgo and the Scarlet Eyes. Finding a map, Kurapika soon arrives at the guild. The agent there informs him that he has not finished his exam, thus she refuses to accept him. He is forced to return to his hotel room.

About a week later, multiple people soon believe him to be another person dine-and-dashing at Burger Kong. This results in him fleeing into a nearby alleyway. There, he encounters Clario once more. She begs him to help her, only for Tron and his men to appear, causing Clario to hide. Tron mistakes the boy for Clario at first. However, after Kurapika attempts to attack him, Tron realizes his mistake and fights him. After Kurapika's defeat, his eyes suddenly turn red. This results in Tron and his men leaving the scene. After they leave, Kurapika confronts Clario about what happened and removes her hood. He is shocked to discover they look the exact same. This results in him realizing she had been the one dine-and-dashing prior. Thus, the duo returns to Burger Kong to apologize. The restaurant owner reveals that the fees had been paid off by the police. Kurapika and Clario proceed to eat together.

The duo later arrives at hotel. There, Clario is asked in regard to Tron's power. She is unable to answer due to not understanding, but reveals that he was trained by a hunter. She also mentions that Rorgo will be attending an upcoming VIP party, but refuses to talk about him further. In the morning, Kurapika awakes to a note left by the princess, revealing her to have left. In the alleys, he runs into Tron's subordinates, who mistake him for Clario. There, they tell him more information about Rorgo and their own boss. At the town fountain is Tron. There, the Kurta member confronts him on both Clario and his power. He learns that she is a princess who was to attend the same event as Rorgo. Kurapika himself is thus asked to come to the hotel. He agrees and is given a bracelet. Tron is then revealed to be a Nen user. Thus, he decides to briefly train the Kurta member, beginning his progress with the Secret Exam.

After awakening his aura nodes, Kurapika begins to seek out someone else who can continue to teach him Nen. He begins hearing about a Hunter named Sansho. Returning to the bar, Kurapika inquires the bartender on both Rorgo and Sansho. He explains that Rorgo is a wealthy man who works alongside Don Gappai. On the other hand, Sansho is a money-obsessed man who claims to be a Hunter. The latter must be contacted at a local Cyber Cafe first. As the bartender lacks the specific password, the Kurta member explores the streets once more until he finds a young girl.

She claims a Noggin Lugging Tortoise is rampaging outside the city. Her father also plans to deal with it. To ease her concerns about said father, Kurapika agrees to deal with the beast in exchange for information on Sansho. Once the deed is done, he is told the password ‘FAKE.’ He is then able to contact and arrange a meeting with Sansho at the local mansion. There, Kurapika promptly requests further training from him. The supposed Hunter agrees so long as he gives him his license. As Kurapika is about to, he suddenly changes his mind. This enrages him, resulting in the Kurta member realizing he’s not an actual Hunter. Sansho confirms this, stating that he failed the Hunter Exam because of Hisoka.

A fight promptly begins. Kurapika at first struggles, but after concentrating his aura, he is soon able to defeat him. A cornered Sansho threatens him, saying that if he were to kill him, the other Heavenly Kings would go after him—namely Rorgo. Instead of scaring him, this has the opposite effect of causing Kurapika’s eyes to become scarlet. Sansho reveals that Rorgo does, in fact, have the Scarlet Eyes and is one of the city’s mafiosos. Kurapika decides to spare him and leaves.

Sometime after, Kurapika returns to the guild. The receptionist notes that Kurapika had stopped Sansho. As thanks, she briefly helps him with his Nen. She then mentions another Fake Hunter roaming around and asks Kurapika to deal with him. Kurapika reluctantly agrees. After finding a loose flyer, Kurapika manages to track down the other Fake Hunter. Said Fake Hunter is Majtani. After recognizing him, Kurapika questions him on how he escaped prison. He reveals that someone had helped him escape. From this, he was given cyborg improvements and became what he calls “Majtani 2.” After this, Kurapika proceeds to fight and defeat Majtani. He then tells him to stop his involvement with crime, lest he fight him again. Kurapika returns to the guild, where the receptionist helps him again.

Afterwards, Kurapika meets with Tron's subordinates. The group decides to team up to find Clario. They then give the Kurta member their contact information in the event he finds her. In the night, Kurapika ends up finding a starving Clario. They eat together and begin heading to the hotel. Clario then talks to Kurapika, commenting on how strict her home life is. She claims to be jealous of Kurapika, who became a hunter by his own choice; this results in Kurapika becoming upset. At the hotel, Kurapika calls Tron, only for Clario to escape. Kurapika eventually finds her. He brings her into the nearby storage warehouse to prevent her escape. However, she then notices a spider. This has Kurapika hit the pipe where it is, leaving him blinded and distracted enough for Clario to run away.

After this, Tron and his men are only left with Kurapika. They decide on one last resort: disguising Kurapika as Clario. Kurapika refuses at first. However, Tron claims to know someone who could properly teach Kurapika Nen. If the boy helps with the VIPs, Tron will contact him. Thus, he agrees and pretends to be her during the hotel party. This results in him meeting Rorgo. Kurapika asks him about his Scarlet Eye collection. Rorgo describes it as a hobby of his and is surprised that the country’s princess knows of it. Kurapika soon excuses himself and eventually finds Clario hiding at the cake table. He tells Tron. From this, Clario is chased after up until she accidentally rips up Rorgo’s shirt, revealing a solid spider tattoo. This causes Kurapika’s eyes to become scarlet. Unbeknownst to him, Rorgo notices this.

While Kurapika learns Nen from Izunavi, the latter is called by Tron, and it's revealed that Clario has gone missing. Kurapika returns to Sunaka to talk with Tron. The duo concludes that Rorgo kidnapped Clario. However, Tron is unable to find any information regarding his whereabouts. This leaves Kurapika to investigate. He is soon able to find his hidden mansion at the outskirts of the city. After defeating Rorgo’s henchmen and going through various hazards, Kurapika finds her locked away. He then frees her. However, this triggers a trap that causes the building to collapse. They are forced to run away. Outside, Rorgo finds them. Kurapika attempts to use Chain Jail. Because it lacks any restrictions, it has no effect on Rorgo. Suddenly, Tron and Izunavi step in. They defend the two teens, leaving Rorgo to declare he’ll be waiting for Kurapika at Logos Island.

Tron and Clario thank Kurapika at the hotel. Kurapika, however, says he needs to fight Rorgo. As he leaves though, Clario asks if they’ll see each other again. He refuses to answer. On the outskirts of town, Kurapika continues training with Izunavi. He insists on creating unbreakable chains. However, Izunavi claims it to be impossible. When Kurapika attempts to leave though, Izunavi brings up the concept of vows and Nen restrictions. With this, Kurapika decides on his Nen restrictions and trains for a week. He then heads for Logos Island.

After his fight with Rorgo, Kurapika returns to the guild to properly find work as a contract hunter. Once he properly joins the Nostrade Family, he meets Clario and Tron again. During their conversation, Clario asks if they’ll see each other again. Kurapika says no. He then wishes her well and leaves as the girl cries out to him.

In Hisoka’s route, he comes to Sunaka both for Phantom Troupe matters and to follow Machi. As he explores the city, he finds a Six-legged Flying Beast rampaging in the streets. He promptly fights it out of boredom. Continuing to explore, he notices a hooligan attacking a woman and decides to defeat said hooligan. The woman attempts to thank him, but he leaves before she can. Soon enough, he is found by Sansho, who fights him as revenge for the exam but is easily defeated by Hisoka, and he then flees.

Later on, Sansho returns with multiple Majtani clones created by Donal. He has the army fight Hisoka. Despite being outnumbered, he defeats them all. Sansho flees once more. Now intrigued, he goes to the Sengi Guild to use their terminal. He looks up information both on Gon and Sansho. The latter, he finds out, typically resides in Dore. Hisoka recalls Uvogin to be there. He decides to regroup and meet with Uvogin, Shizuku, and Chrollo. 

Trivia

The town's bar has the Hunter Website Bartender tending the establishment where he gives hints. He is referred to as マスター (Masutaa, literally master * ).
Sengi Guild's agent is named レイ Hunter × Hunter: Each One's Determination (Rei), making it the only instance where she has one.
In Kurapika’s 3rd chapter, there is an optional easter egg where he can find Machi and Shizuku exploring the town.
In Hisoka’s 4th chapter, there is an optional easter egg where he can find Neon.

Intertextuality and References

Burger Kong is a parody name of the fast-food franchise Burger King.
